title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: Let $G$ be a graph, and let $F:V(G)\xrightarrow{}2^{\mathbb{N}}$. If $|F(v)|<\frac{1}{2}\deg(v)$ for each $v\in V(G)$, then $G$ has an $F$-avoiding orientation.
context_text: Akbari, Dalirrooyfard, Ehsani, Ozeki, and Sherkati introduced this conjecture in the study of orientations avoiding forbidden out-degree lists. They established the statement when $\frac{1}{2}$ is replaced by $\frac{1}{4}$. The sharpness of the $\frac{1}{2}$ bound is witnessed by $K_{2k+1}$ with $F(v)=\{k,k+1,\dots,2k-1\}$.
attributed_to: Akbari, Dalirrooyfard, Ehsani, Ozeki, and Sherkati
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture under the stronger bound $|F(v)|<\lfloor\frac{1}{3}\deg(v)\rfloor$ for all graphs, and further improves the coefficient to $\sqrt{2}-1-o(1)\approx 0.414$ when the maximum degree is subexponential in terms of the minimum degree.
notes: The condition $|F(v)|<\frac{1}{2}\deg(v)$ was truncated in the extracted theorem environment; it is completed here from the abstract and surrounding context.

title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: Let $G$ be a graph, and let $F:V(G)\xrightarrow{}2^{\mathbb{N}}$. If $G$ has an orientation $D$ such that $|F(v)|\leq\deg_{D}^{+}(v)$ for every $v\in V(G)$, then $G$ has an $F$-avoiding orientation.
context_text: This companion conjecture from Akbari et al. relates the size of each forbidden list to the out-degree of the corresponding vertex in a fixed reference orientation rather than to its total degree. It is motivated by the fact that every graph admits an orientation with $\deg_D^+(v)\geq\lfloor\deg_G(v)/2\rfloor$, which would make Conjecture 1.2 a strengthening of Conjecture 1.1.
attributed_to: Akbari, Dalirrooyfard, Ehsani, Ozeki, and Sherkati
paper_contribution: The paper's Combinatorial Nullstellensatz-based sufficient condition (Theorem 1.6) applies to both conjectures and yields new partial results toward each.
notes: The condition in the extracted theorem environment was truncated; the quantitative hypothesis is reconstructed from the surrounding context describing the motivation for Conjecture 1.2.

title: Question 6.1
statement_text: Does every graph $G$ have a spanning subgraph with an Alon-Tarsi orientation in which each vertex $v$ has out-degree at least $\frac{1}{2}(\deg_{G}(v)-1)$?
context_text: This question asks whether Conjecture 1.1 could be proved for every graph via the paper's Theorem 1.4 by finding a suitable Alon-Tarsi orientation, which would yield a stronger result. An affirmative answer would further imply that every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices and maximum degree $\Delta$ has a subgraph $H$ with at least $|E(G)|-n/2$ edges satisfying $\operatorname{AT}(H)\leq\lfloor\frac{1}{2}\Delta+\frac{3}{2}\rfloor$.

title: Question 6.2
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $C$ such that every graph $G$ of maximum degree $\Delta$ has a matching $M$ such that $\operatorname{AT}(G-M)\leq\frac{1}{2}\Delta+C$?
context_text: This question is motivated by results showing that removing a matching from a graph can reduce upper bounds on coloring parameters such as the Alon-Tarsi number. It is noted that $C$ cannot be smaller than $\frac{1}{2}$, since for any odd $n$ and any maximum matching $M$ in $K_n$, $\operatorname{AT}(K_n-M)=\frac{n+1}{2}$. The authors ask specifically about matchings, inspired by Zhu's result on planar graphs and the result of Grytczuk and Zhu.
